cards.question: Page 21 in 'Lesson 1: Configuring Infor M3 Financial Accounting' (Financial Accounting features) - detail 1: Explain 'Lesson 1: Configuring Infor M3 Financial' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Lesson 1: Configuring Infor M3 Financial Accounting Financial Accounting features Infor M3 Financial Accounting includes features and programs to assist in the daily processing and coding of business transactions that feed into the general ledger. They include: • Financial Application Management (FAM) functions • Accounting rules (based on accounting events and accounting types) FAM functions The term FAM is short for Financial Application Management. The good way to describe a FAM function is workflow. The purpose of the FAM function is to make the entry of new transactions as easy and automated as possible. A FAM function does this by collecting settings or rules specific for certain types of entry. Transactions are recorded based on the methods, terms, and voucher number series specified in the FAM function selected. FAM functions are defined in FAM Function. Open (CRS405). cards.goodAnswer: Lesson 1: Configuring Infor M3 Financial Accounting Configuration overview The following is a high-level list of the components that must be defined prior to using Infor M3 Financial Accounting. • A company must be entered in Company. Open (MNS095). • A division for the company must be entered in Division. Open (MNS100). If the company has no divisions, a blank must be entered. • At least one system language must be set in Language. Open (CRS010). • Periods must be defined per period type and the period type must be set for the financial system in Division. Open (MNS100). • The system calendar must be defined in System Calendar. Open (CRS900). • System periods must be established in System Calendar. Open Period (CRS910). • Users must be set up in User. Open (MNS150). • A facility must exist or be created in Facility. Open (CRS008). • At least one warehouse must exist or be defined in Warehouse. Open (MMS005). In addition, the following

cards.question: Page 22 in 'Lesson 1: Configuring Infor M3 Financial Accounting' (Configuration overview) - detail 2: Explain 'There are three key currency types' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: There are three key currency types: local currency, foreign currency, and a third currency. o Define currencies: Currency. Open (CRS055) o Define exchange rate types: Exchange Rate Type. Open (CRS056) o Define exchange rates: Exchange Rate. Open Rate Type (CRS057) • Define chart of accounts Each financial transaction in Infor M3 is recorded based on an accounting string. This string consists of seven links, so-called accounting dimensions. Not all links need to be used for every transaction. The first accounting dimension is always reserved for the business account. cards.goodAnswer: Lesson 1: Configuring Infor M3 Financial Accounting A primary requirement of a financial system is providing the means to access information quickly and easily. In Infor M3, you collect information in a systematic way in the General Ledger balance file by using accounting structures and balance keys. The General Ledger stores detail transactions. The balance file stores totals per periods. Each time the General Ledger is updated, the balance file is updated simultaneously. By using the balance file for inquiries, the reply times are shortened. The balance file is available in GL Balance File. Display (GLS215) and in the Infor M3 Report Generator. o Define accounting structure levels o Define accounting structures o Define balance keys o Define column templates for balance inquiry • Enable General Ledger management Some of the basic functions in the financial system are configured in a separate program for General Ledger settings. o Basi

A divisional structure can be defined with the following considerations in mind: • Current company’s legal entity structure • Balance Sheet Requirements • Tax Structure • Region • Currency Requirements • Fiscal Calendar • Consolidation Requirements • Banking Information and Integrations This course adopts the following organizational structure: Example of an organizational structure Note: In the cloud we recommend that one company is utilized for the production tenant. cards.question: Page 34 in 'Lesson 1: Configuring Infor M3 Financial Accounting' (System calendar and periods) - detail 1: Explain 'Lesson 1: Configuring Infor M3 Financial' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Lesson 1: Configuring Infor M3 Financial Accounting System calendar and periods A system calendar must be defined for each of the divisions included in the configuration. This means that a calendar must be defined for the central (blank) division, even if the division is not used to process information. When a date is entered in Infor M3, the system checks that the date has been defined in the system calendar. System periods are used to enter different kinds of data that use different periods. Up to five systems periods can be entered for each specified division and for the blank division. At least one seasonal curve can be entered for each specified division and for the blank division. Configure the system calendar The System Calendar. Open (CRS900) program is the foundation for everything date-related in Infor M3. From a financial perspective, the system calendar must be generated for at least the current year and the next two years.

cards.question: Page 37 in 'Lesson 1: Configuring Infor M3 Financial Accounting' (Number series) - detail 1: Explain 'Lesson 1: Configuring Infor M3 Financial' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Lesson 1: Configuring Infor M3 Financial Accounting Number series Number series are used for assigning unique identities to each individual order or transaction in Infor M3. They are significant because they are used in countless areas of Infor M3. Number series are beneficial to the end user as identification criteria. Journal number series When you exit a program that creates accounting transactions, one or several journals are printed, depending on the program. Journal examples include accounting journals, customer invoice journals, and supplier payment journals. Each journal is assigned a number from the journal number series. In the journal, every transaction receives a sequence number. The sequence number gives each transaction a unique identity in the general ledger and orders the transactions chronologically for Infor M3-internal purposes only. If you have configured transactions separated into external and internal transactions

cards.goodAnswer: Each number series has a name, starting number, ending number, and a setting for if, and when, the series is replaced by another series. Each number series is connected to a number series type that is predefined and indicates what the series is used for (such as for customer/service orders, for invoices, for delivery notes, and for payment reminders). Most number series types are defined in Number Series. Open (CRS165). Invoice number series Internal invoice number series are created in Internal Invoice Series. Divisions are automatically connected to number series 02. Different invoices with the same number can be entered in separate divisions. When a company consists of two or more legal units as separate Infor M3 divisions and goods are distributed from one warehouse to another, the scenario type determines which accounting rules to apply in some accounting events. cards.question: Page 39 in 'Lesson 1: Configuring Infor M3 Financial Accounting' (Vouchers) - detail 1: Explain 'Lesson 1: Configuring Infor M3 Financial' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Lesson 1: Configuring Infor M3 Financial Accounting Vouchers Building logic into voucher numbers is not recommended. All relevant information is found in the system. For example, it does not add any useful information to add the year to the voucher number. Infor M3 will not accept the same voucher number used twice. Good practice allows using the full range of numbers available. The sooner the system runs out of voucher numbers, the sooner archiving must be performed. Archiving is the Infor M3 term for when the system is cleared of old, obsolete transactions to improve performance and to facilitate reusing old voucher numbers. At least one voucher number series is needed. Voucher number series are set up with a valid from date, so a new series can be created and activated from a certain date. It is normal practice to create at least one voucher number series per module. Books of account Voucher number series can be converted to books of

cards.goodAnswer: Lesson 1: Configuring Infor M3 Financial Accounting User and language settings To use Infor M3, each user must be defined as a user. The user profile in Infor M3 contains all the environmental information that is assigned to each user to set up the correct environment when the user starts a new session. This includes the default values for the company and division, language, date format, etc. for a user. The user can override some, but not all, settings within the session. Languages are very important and must be defined within Infor M3 before text can be used in applicable fields. Infor M3 provides a process for entering certain field descriptions in alternate languages in various master record programs. This functionality is particularly useful in instances where the system is implemented in multiple countries. After a master record is created in a base language, you can update the record by entering an alternate description in one or

cards.goodAnswer: Lesson 1: Configuring Infor M3 Financial Accounting Currency settings You can use any number of currencies in Infor M3. There are three key currency types: • Local currency: Local currency is the base, domestic currency used by the company and to which all transactions are converted in the General Ledger. A value expressed in this currency is called local amount, although sometimes referred to as functional currency. • Foreign currency: Foreign currency is any other currency used. A value expressed in such a currency is called currency amount. o A foreign currency can be referred to as the transactional currency. This currency can be the same as or deviate from the local currency. A purchase or a sale may have a different currency than the local. Accounts Payable (AP) and Accounts Receivable (AR) subledgers can be portrayed in this currency when displaying the balance in Infor M3 screens (i.e., APS200/APS225 and ARS200/ARS225) ▪ For exa

Such groups enable you to categorize your banks according to your own definitions. Bank groups are defined on Bank Group. Open (CRS698). Consider: Bank groups are mainly used for cash flow management purposes. By grouping banks for which you have negotiated special terms regarding interest or fees, for example, you can more easily identify the banks that offer the best terms in specific situations. VAT registration number The VAT registration number is used by companies within European Union (EU) to account for sales (trade) within EU without VAT being charged. cards.goodAnswer: Lesson 1: Configuring Infor M3 Financial Accounting Application messages This topic explains how to set up the internal Infor M3 application message system. If activated, users responsible for performing certain tasks will receive an application message when changes occur regarding, for example, invoices to be authorized or recoded, or if any problems occurred when an invoice was matched with the purchase order and goods receipt. Users can access their Infor M3 application messages via e-mail or short message service (SMS), and can modify their settings to give other users the authority to deal with one or several message types over a certain period. For example, a user who is responsible for authorizing supplier invoices might authorize someone else to receive these messages during an absence from the office. cards.question: Page 62 in 'Lesson 2: Accounting identities and Accounting rules' (Account groups) - detail 1: Explain 'Lesson 2: Accounting identities and Accounting' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Lesson 2: Accounting identities and Accounting rules Account groups Account groups are used to further categorize account codes. The groups can then be used for reporting or analysis. They are defined at the company and division level. Each account group must be defined for inclusion in the statement of income or the balance sheet. There are five levels of account groups. The levels determine the order in which accounts within an account group are listed on an income statement of income or balance sheet. Each level is assigned a sequence number. This number determines in which order a specific level and connected account groups are printed. Accounts are sorted in the following order: 1) Level, 2) Sequence number, 3) Account group. Account groups are defined and maintained in Account Group. Open (CRS633). When defining dimension (account codes) in Accounting Identity. Open (CRS630) the account group field is mandatory. cards.goodAnswer: Lesson 2: Accounting identities and Accounting rules Accounting rules overview Accounting rule composition An accounting rule is a is a fully defined accounting string Infor M3 uses when account entries are automatically created in a financial activity. Accounting rules are set up to avoid manual transaction entry and minimize key-in errors. This is critical because of the volume of transactions that occur in a fully integrated system. As many as 75-80% of the transactions in Infor M3 Accounts Payable are generated by Infor M3 itself rather than by human hand. Accounting rules are paramount to making those transactions come out correctly. They control what transactions are created when a certain event occurs. Most of the integration between the financial system and other Infor M3 applications is managed by the accounting rules. For example, if accounting dimension in an accounting rule is reserved for product groups, enter the ID of the item group field in the item master file. The product group is then automatically retrieved from this file. Exceptions In addition, exceptions can be set up to apply in certain circumstances. An example of an exception would be the use of a different control account where the supplier forms part of a particular supplier group. Each accounting rule can have up to ten exceptions. An example could be the use of a different revenue account for non-domestic revenues. cards.goodAnswer: Lesson 3: Accounts Payable overview Accounts Payable overview The Infor M3 Accounts Payable process flow provides users with a powerful tool to manage all aspects of the Accounts Payable cycle from invoice checking and control to invoice payment and supplier monitoring. Accounts Payable process overview Infor M3 includes these functions for each area of the accounts payable process: • Preparing/entering supplier invoices for payment: This includes the automated and manual entry of supplier invoices, authorizations, and approvals. Invoice matching to PO and goods receipt is integrated in the entry flow. • Paying suppliers: This includes the automated payment routines, manual payments (both full and partial), and recurring payments. cards.goodAnswer: Lesson 4: Configuring Accounts Payable Supplier setup Local currency exception When creating a supplier, first a domestic supplier master record will be created. The domestic supplier will be assigned a currency code depending on the division where the supplier is used. For example, if a United States supplier is created, it will be assigned a currency of United States dollars (USD). Note: Even though it was created as a US currency, this supplier master record is visible to all divisions of a company. If a supplier works with more than one division, Infor recommends that only one supplier exists for all divisions and if local exceptions exist, such as currency code, VAT code, payment terms, and so forth, the supplier be created with local exceptions by division. Open (CRS620/E) Field Description The VAT registration number is used by companies within the EU to account for sales (trade) within the EU without VAT being charged. EU regulations state that VAT should be charged when invoicing a customer within the EU if the registration number is not known, that is, it is up to the salesperson to verify the registration number of the customer (payer) to avoid charging VAT. Because each member country within the EU has its own registration number series for this purpose, validation of the entered registration number cannot be made. However, this registration number should always be seen with the country as a prefix, which makes the identity unique. © Infor. All rights reserved. © Infor. All rights reserved. © Infor. cards.goodAnswer: Lesson 4: Configuring Accounts Payable Settings – Accounts Payable (APS905/F) Field Description Auto match of credit notes on payment proposal Select this check box to match credit transactions automatically when a payment proposal is created on Suppl Payment Proposal. Open (APS130). Credit transactions in this context are supplier credit notes, supplier on- account payments, and (if the supplier is also a registered customer) customer invoices to include for netting. If you select this check box, credit transactions are matched against supplier invoices, regardless of payment date, to avoid negative payments. The payment dates of the credit transactions and supplier invoices are automatically changed to the payment date of the last transaction. This procedure only includes transactions of the following types: • Invoices and credit transactions with payment methods with Grouped payment code = (Grouped per due date) on AP Payment Method.

These rules are relevant for three-way matching to PO receipts. This topic explains how to define rules for which variances should be automatically accepted when a difference of price or quantity occurs between the goods receipt lines and the supplier invoice. Open (APS020) When coding invoices, differences (or remaining amounts to distribute) may exist between the invoice amount and the PO or PO line item. To manage these events and enable write-offs, or adjustments, use the program AP Accounting Option. Open (APS020). Within the program, accounting options between the range of and with the required accounting adjustment can be defined. The program also allows you to set up which accounting string to be updated when the accounting rule conditions are met. This program must be maintained at the divisional level. cards.goodAnswer: Lesson 5: Processing supplier invoices Processing supplier invoices overview When an invoice is received from a supplier or other vendor, you must prepare the invoice for payment by authorizing, recoding, and approving the invoice. If the invoice is tied to a PO, it must be matched against a PO and goods receipt. This process can be either manual or automated. Review incoming supplier invoices First, check the paper invoices you receive, according to your company's routines and policy. To simplify the entry and to make it easier to decide which workflow to use, sort all invoices you accept to pay and divide them into categories (for example, approved, unapproved, with or without matching to PO). Manually enter supplier invoices for payment Paper invoices are entered in Supplier Invoice. Record (APS100). You then select the FAM function detail record for the type of invoice that will control much of the workflow. If the invoice contains

cards.goodAnswer: Lesson 5: Processing supplier invoices Post invoices Depending on your settings, you post the invoice either manually or automatically in Supplier Invoice. Record (APS100/J). The recurring payments you entered directly in Suppl Payment. Open Recurring (APS095), without connection to an invoice, are not posted. Manual payments or adjustments on supplier’s account Manual payments are used to record or adjust payments already made. Infor M3 supports several types of manual payments, including: • Full payment • Partial payment • On-account payment • Cash discount • Accounting options Automatically processing supplier invoices for payment An alternative method to manually processing supplier invoices for payments is to automate the process. This is done through displaying batch supplier invoices in Supplier Invoice Batch. Open (APS450). cards.question: Page 171 in 'Lesson 6: Processing supplier payments' (Processing supplier payments overview) - detail 1: Explain 'Lesson 6: Processing supplier payments Processing' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Lesson 6: Processing supplier payments Processing supplier payments overview In Infor M3, a payment proposal is created to prepare a list of invoices that are due for payment. The payment is then processed in full, partial, or on-account. Electronic payments are also handled in the same manner, using a proposal to create the payment request interface file that is then used as input to Infor M3 e-Collaborator for the electronic interchange with the bank or financial instituti on. Infor M3 Financial Accounting also allows companies to perform netting for suppliers that are also defined as customers. A prerequisite is that the Supplier Customer Number field on Supplier. Define Purchase Financial (CRS624) is updated with a valid customer number. In such a case, the supplier's customer invoices will appear as credit notes. For example, when a supplier telephones regarding an order, you will be able to view the order. Infor M3 offers options for displaying different functions, including Accounts Payable. Display (APS200) and Accounts Payable. Display Balances (APS225), among others. These options allow you to choose the inquiry routine that best fits the situation. Inquiries can also be customized for the most common daily monitor transactions or for other “as -needed” scenarios. Infor M3 workspaces In addition to M3 displays, workspaces can be configured to provide immediate attention to alerts on specific events utilizing information monitor widgets. It is good practice to look at the predefined column templates as the basis for your own templates. cards.question: Page 214 in 'Lesson 8: Accounts Receivable overview' (Accounts Receivable process) - detail 1: Explain 'Lesson 8: Accounts Receivable overview Accounts' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Lesson 8: Accounts Receivable overview Accounts Receivable process All businesses have a process to ensure that the customers pay invoices promptly. The Infor M3 solution includes a process called managing accounts receivable and an Infor M3 module called Accounts Receivable. When invoices are created in other areas of Infor M3, they are automatically updated into the Infor M3 Accounts Receivable module for easy monitoring. These invoices can be based on customer orders, service orders, project orders, fixed asset sales, internal invoicing between divisions, and so on. The Accounts Receivable module is a sub-ledger of the General Ledger module that lists money that is owed to your company by a customer for products and services provided on credit. This money is treated as a current asset on the balance sheet. One of the major tasks for any financial department is to make sure that customers pay their invoices promptly and without delay.

cards.question: Page 215 in 'Lesson 8: Accounts Receivable overview' (Accounts Receivable process) - detail 1: Explain 'Lesson 8: Accounts Receivable overview Accounts' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Lesson 8: Accounts Receivable overview Accounts Receivable process flow Processing customer payments The Infor M3 Accounts Receivable module enables the company to manage the many payment variants used in international trade, including electronic bank transfers, drafts with and without acceptance, checks and postdated checks, direct debiting, and factoring. Using direct debiting The purpose of direct debiting is to automatically transfer specific amounts from the customer's bank account to the company bank account at certain intervals. Direct debiting is primarily used when selling the same services or products on a regular basis to a customer. Through direct debiting a company can automate the process of invoicing customers and recording payments Selling receivables to factoring company Infor M3 supports the outsourcing of collection of the company’s customer payments to a factoring company, either temporarily or permanently. cards.question: Page 216 in 'Lesson 8: Accounts Receivable overview' (Accounts Receivable process) - detail 1: Explain 'Lesson 8: Accounts Receivable overview Grouping' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Lesson 8: Accounts Receivable overview Grouping invoices Replace multiple customer invoices sent to one customer with one group invoice. The customer can then pay against the group invoice instead of paying each invoice separately. Group invoices enable customers to minimize the administrative work surrounding payments. When payment is received for a group invoice, the amount paid is automatically allocated to the invoices included in the group invoice. Group invoices by automatically selecting all invoices that match selection criteria or by manually selecting invoices to include. You can also group invoices for internal purposes to keep track of invoices and payments related to a certain operation or transaction. Manually creating invoices and invoice records There are two different types of customer invoices, manual invoicing and customer invoice entry. cards.goodAnswer: Lesson 9: Configuring Accounts Receivable Settings – Accounts Receivable (ARS905/E) Field Description – SEPA DD CORE This field indicates the number of days before the collection date that a Single Euro Payments Area (SEPA) direct debiting proposal can be sent to the bank at the earliest, when the SEPA direct debiting scheme is set to CORE and the sequence type is set to FRST. The payer's bank must receive the request for a direct debit collection at least xx bank days prior to the collection date. The xx days can be different depending on the type of SEPA direct debiting scheme and the sequence type. The collection date is the payment date on an invoice. The payment date can be the due date or the cash discount date. cards.question: Page 250 in 'Lesson 10: Processing customer invoices' (Configuration highlights) - detail 1: Explain 'Lesson 10: Processing customer invoices Configuration' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Lesson 10: Processing customer invoices Configuration highlights AR10 - Work with Customer Invoices (CRS405) The AR10 FAM function is typically used in simple processing or when integrating existing invoices from other systems. Account entries are created when a customer invoice record, for example, a customer opening balance, is created manually in Customer Invoice. Enter (ARS100). Within this FAM function, some of the areas you can define for these processes are: • General voucher text code • Whether it is a cross division voucher • The cash discount method • The invoice category AR20 - Work with Manual Invoicing (CRS405) AR20 is the normal manual invoicing process used with Accounts Receivable. It is more automated than AR10. Create corrective customer invoices The functionality of corrective invoices is mandatory in some countries, for example, in Spain. Corrective invoices are very similar to credit invoices except that it is mandatory to reference the original invoice that is credited when creating corrective invoices. The two methods to create corrective invoices include: • Select the invoice line and change the amount. • Credit all lines of the original invoice and automatically create a new one with the correct amounts. AR30- C Suspense account for VAT payable This rule is used when the VAT reported on payment day is selected in combination with VAT method or in (CRS030). AR30- D Unpaid advances This rule indicates the account that is debited when a project advance is paid. See PO20- (Unpaid advances). AR30- C Paid advances This rule indicates the account that is credited when a project advance is paid. AR30- Accepted payment difference This rule represents the difference between the invoiced and actual paid amount that is accepted and written off automatically, as defined in Payment Tolerance. This number is used as a reference for a specific debt collection proposal. If debt collection proposals are supposed to be created in a blank division, settings must be created and defined in Settings - Accounts Receivable (ARS905). The parameter Debt Collection in the multiple users section must be activated or deactivated both in the blank division and in the active division. cards.question: Page 314 in 'Lesson 13: Configuring General Ledger' (Accounting structure) - detail 1: Explain 'Lesson 13: Configuring General Ledger Accounting' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Lesson 13: Configuring General Ledger Accounting structure An accounting structure is a group of accounting identities or other accounting structures in a hierarchical model corresponding to an organization’s structure. The purpose of accounting structures is to group identities (cost centers, for example) for display and printout purposes. This is especially important when the identities you want to group together are not sequentially numbered. Structures also make it easier to handle a company’s organizational changes. Sub -structures containing many accounting identities can be easily moved to new accounting structures. This has an immediate impact on all reports and queries that are based on the structure. cards.question: Page 316 in 'Lesson 13: Configuring General Ledger' (Addition table) - detail 1: Explain 'Lesson 13: Configuring General Ledger Addition' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Lesson 13: Configuring General Ledger Addition table An addition table is used to replace the original account string in, or add a missing value in, dimension - 7. Replacement could be used, for example, when a cost center is retrieved from a PO, but the cost center on the PO is different from the cost center required in the General Ledger. When the General Ledger is updated, the original value from the PO is replaced by the cost center set in GLS020. Addition could be used, for example, when a local legal chart of account is required as well as the corporate chart of account. The addition table would add the legal account into a specific dimension based on the corporate account used, saving the user the time to enter both. Demo 13.2: Create an addition table This demonstration shows how to create an addition table. Before you begin: • Ensure you are logged in to the OS Portal and Infor M3. cards.goodAnswer: Lesson 13: Configuring General Ledger Balance keys To sort the aggregated values by period in the balance file, Infor M3 uses balance keys. Balance keys define what information is stored in the balance file and the order in which the information is displayed. Eight predefined balance keys are delivered with the system. • Balance key number sorts the information by account only (the first accounting dimension). • Balance key number sorts the balance file by the second accounting dimension, then by account. • The eight predefined keys sort the information by variants of the standard accounting dimensions. Balance keys can be created at any time with the GL Balance File Key. Open (GLS690) program and then populated by filtering the detailed information in the General Ledger file into the new balance key. cards.goodAnswer: Lesson 14: Processing General Ledger Daily routines Journal vouchers Journal vouchers are used to enter transactions not created in any other function in Infor M3. Examples of journal vouchers are vouchers for temporary adjustments (temporary vouchers), bank transactions, reservations, and year-end transactions for adjusting annual financial statements (period 13). The process can also be used to enter customer/supplier payments and customer/supplier invoices. Exercise 14.1: Create a journal voucher In this exercise, you will create a standard voucher from the FAM function you created earlier. Before you begin: • Ensure you are logged in to the OS Portal and Infor M3. If not, refer to Exercise 1.1. • Ensure you have completed all previous exercises because they provide data or configurations for this exercise. Types of standard vouchers in Infor M3 include: • Complete voucher: A complete voucher contains all the accounting lines and amounts necessary. Debits and credits must be balanced. cards.question: Page 350 in 'Lesson 15: Monitoring General Ledger' (General Ledger balance file) - detail 1: Explain 'Lesson 15: Monitoring General Ledger General' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Lesson 15: Monitoring General Ledger General Ledger balance file One of the main requirements of a financial system is that a company can access detailed as well as aggregated financial information quickly and easily. Infor M3 collects and aggregates information in a systematic way in the General Ledger balance file. Financial reports in Infor M3 are generated from the balance file through the report generator function. While the General Ledger stores detail transactions, the balance file stores totals per period. When the General Ledger is updated, the balance file is also updated. The function GL Balance File. Display (GLS215) is used to access the information. That information can then be displayed in different ways through balance keys and column templates. Infor M3 uses the balance file for inquiries because the response times are shorter than in the report generator. The balance file is also used to calculate opening balances on G
